items.front: What must controllers protect against when aircraft cross an offshore or oceanic boundary below FL180?
items.back: Ensure differences between QNE and QNH altimeter settings do not compromise vertical separation.

items.front: When does course divergence establish separation as aircraft enter oceanic airspace?
items.back: When the aircraft are on courses diverging by at least 15 degrees, radar separation exists and is increasing at the edge of known radar coverage, and oceanic lateral separation will follow.

items.front: When may an aircraft climb through opposite-direction oceanic traffic during transition from offshore to oceanic airspace?
items.back: Only when vertical separation above the opposite-direction traffic is established before boundary crossing and 15 minutes before the aircraft are estimated to pass.

items.front: Under the same-direction offshore-to-oceanic climb procedure, when must the following aircraft begin climbing?
items.back: It must commence climb within 10 minutes after passing the required reported or radar-observed position of the preceding aircraft.

items.front: When may radar standards be applied before a transitioning aircraft is radar identified?
items.back: Only under the listed transition conditions, and not if there is insufficient time to establish another approved separation method should prompt radar identification fail.
